headline: Artemis II astronauts splash down off California, ending historic Moon mission
description: Following a brief but nerve-wracking communications blackout during their high-stakes re-entry on Friday, mission commander Wiseman's voice triggered relief that the astronauts were well on their way back home. After leaving the bobbing capsule, they will be flown to a recovery ship off the Pacific coast near San Diego.

headline: Lebanon says talks with Israel scheduled for Tuesday in the US
Macron and Pope Leo XIV call for Middle East dialogue
Iranians express bitterness at war: 'The jailer hasn't changed, my friends, and we are all in line for death'
description: In accounts gathered by Le Monde, residents describe enduring record-high inflation and intensified political repression, in addition to the war. The current lull seems fragile and uncertain to them.

headline: Djibouti's President Ismail Omar Guelleh claims sixth-term victory
description: The 78-year-old leader, who was has ruled the small nation for 27 years with an iron grip, declared in a social media post on Saturday that he had been 'reelected'. Early results gave him a huge lead over his little-known opponent in Friday's election.

headline: Paris mayor Emmanuel Grégoire seeks to 'unblock' talks over sale of Parc des Princes to PSG
description: The new mayor of Paris has called a Council meeting for April 14, during which he will ask the city's 163 elected officials to authorize the start of negotiations with the football club over the stadium's future.

headline: A French judge was found burned at a cliff in Djibouti in 1995. How and why he died remains a mystery
description: On October 19, 1995, the body of Bernard Borrel was found burned at the base of a cliff in Djibouti. More than 30 years later, his widow Elisabeth Borrel continues her fight to shed light on the case, which has become entangled in military interests, diplomatic pressure and state secrets.

description: The Israeli military launched its largest offensive against Hezbollah on Wednesday. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu fears Donald Trump could pressure him into accepting a rushed agreement with Iran.

headline: Three writers on how Simone de Beauvoir influenced their relationship with feminism
description: For the 40th anniversary of the philosopher's death, French writers Louise Chennevière, Pauline Delabroy-Allard and Blandine Rinkel share how Beauvoir has impacted them with Le Monde.
byline: Blandine Rinkel ,  Louise Chennevière and  Pauline Delabroy-Allard

headline: Macron to meet Pope Leo XIV on Friday to discuss a world in crisis
description: The French president and the American pontiff are expected to address conflicts in Lebanon and Iran and contemporary issues such as the dangers posed by the development of artificial intelligence.
